user_message: Working on Iron Creek Mining's forecast prep. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me build the analysis.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 11:19 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 11:46 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 08:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining forecast prep

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-J trap (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-J failure mode (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes'))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-J -- Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')
